meaning: adj.任性的,暴躁的,[航海]摇荡不稳的,怪僻的
examples: Vegetarianism has shed its cranky image.
Who can blame them for being cranky?
Thanks to an exhausted and cranky state, for which there is no prospect of dramatic reform, much of the job of development will fall upon the private sector.
Most Americans would disagree with my view - some might even regard it as cranky.
But that hasn't stemmed people's enthusiasm for the small cat with the cranky face.
